run_command: @'<inline color calibration reference diagnostic script>'@ | python -
main_issues: The current color model already samples crop margins, but it uses mixed 94th/2nd percentiles instead of explicitly classifying physical references.
Current film_base_rgb is close to the classified clear-film-base candidate on all three complete rolls, so film-edge calibration is a good direction.
Current black_reference_rgb is a very dark scanner or border value around 0.046 in all channels; it is stable but should not be used as a full white-point solution.
The next color model should classify clear film base separately from dark edge/scanner pixels, then use in-frame highlight density for the positive white scale.
next_step: Replace the current mixed-margin percentile color model with explicit film-edge reference classification, keeping the old percentile behavior as fallback when classification is weak.
